target.name: Galvanick
target.founder: Joshua Steinman, Brandon Park, Feliks Pleszczynski

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4.4B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galvanick operates in Defense & Security at the Seed stage, having raised $10M+. The valuation universe comprises 10 peers across public and private markets: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with notable comparables including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The analysis applied one quantitative method. Stage-Progression valuation uses a 2.5x step-up multiplier applied to a $4.4B total-raised proxy, implying a post-Seed valuation of $11.0B. Peer-Median comparison was not applicable; while 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than three datapoints, insufficient for reliable median estimation.

The triangulated range is $9.3B (low) to $12.6B (high), with a median of $11.0B. This range reflects stage-appropriate progression assumptions for frontier-tech defense infrastructure. Key uncertainties include limited comparable transaction data at Galvanick's specific stage and the nascent nature of several peer valuations in this sector, both of which constrain confidence in the peer-derived bounds. The valuation remains sensitive to Seed-to-Series A step multiples and market conditions in defense-tech funding.
